sections.Description:
Arachnid Psions are identifiable by their squid-like cyan body and pink bumps. While alive, they grant all other Vek except for anyBlobs,Spores,SpiderlingsandSpiderling Eggsthe ability to spawn a Spiderling Egg on death. The Spiderling Egg matches those that spawn from theSpider Leaderand does not web surrounding tiles. These eggs spawn even if the vek falls into water or a bottomless pit, with the egg flying out of the body of water or hole and landing on a nearby land tile.
Psions can only spawn once per mission. ThePsionic Receiverwill force disabled Mechs to spawn enemy Spiderling Eggs, so it has no direct benefit to the player.[1]
sections.Strategy:
While the spiderling eggs are of no direct harm, they can be nuisance when spawned from many defeated enemies as they can block grounded Mech movement. Additionally, multiplespiderlingsin the next enemy movement turn can quickly overwhelm the Grid if they are allowed to spread out. Thus, it is recommended to kill the Arachnid Psion as early as possible.
If taking out the Arachnid Psion is not possible due to other enemies threatening the grid, look for ways to displace enemies without killing them. Or alternatively, enemies can be killed on top of hazardous tiles including Fire, Electric Smoke, incoming Vek spawns, or environmental hazards such as Lightning Strikes; the incoming damage will immediately dispose of the Spiderling Egg.
Of note, squads that are effective at countering the Arachnid Psion are those which have access to passive damaging effects and wide area targeting weapons. These includeBlitzkriegwith Lightning Whip,Flame Behemothsto create fire andRusting Hulkswith electric smoke.Missile Barrageis also a very effective weapon at defeating multiple spiderlings.
sections.Trivia: Before version 1.2.82, Vek that fell into liquid or chasm tiles could spawn their flying spider egg on top of time pods, crushing it in the process.

The

Description
[
]
Arachnid Psions are identifiable by their squid-like cyan body and pink bumps. While alive, they grant all other Vek except for any
Blobs
,
Spores
,
Spiderlings
and
Spiderling Eggs
the ability to spawn a Spiderling Egg on death. The Spiderling Egg matches those that spawn from the
Spider Leader
and does not web surrounding tiles. These eggs spawn even if the vek falls into water or a bottomless pit, with the egg flying out of the body of water or hole and landing on a nearby land tile.
Psions can only spawn once per mission. The
Psionic Receiver
will force disabled Mechs to spawn enemy Spiderling Eggs, so it has no direct benefit to the player.
[
1
]
Strategy
[
]
While the spiderling eggs are of no direct harm, they can be nuisance when spawned from many defeated enemies as they can block grounded Mech movement. Additionally, multiple
spiderlings
in the next enemy movement turn can quickly overwhelm the Grid if they are allowed to spread out. Thus, it is recommended to kill the Arachnid Psion as early as possible.
If taking out the Arachnid Psion is not possible due to other enemies threatening the grid, look for ways to displace enemies without killing them. Or alternatively, enemies can be killed on top of hazardous tiles including Fire, Electric Smoke, incoming Vek spawns, or environmental hazards such as Lightning Strikes; the incoming damage will immediately dispose of the Spiderling Egg.
Of note, squads that are effective at countering the Arachnid Psion are those which have access to passive damaging effects and wide area targeting weapons. These include
Blitzkrieg
with Lightning Whip,
Flame Behemoths
to create fire and
Rusting Hulks
with electric smoke.
Missile Barrage
is also a very effective weapon at defeating multiple spiderlings.
Trivia
[
]
Before version 1.2.82, Vek that fell into liquid or chasm tiles could spawn their flying spider egg on top of time pods, crushing it in the process.
